title: Antirrhinum fruticosum
intro: Antirrhinum fruticosum, also known as the downy toadflax or tree toadflax, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the figwort family (Scrophulariaceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including its ability to treat digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Antirrhinum fruticosum include iridoid glycosides, phenolic compounds, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Antirrhinum fruticosum is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to treat various health conditions, including gastrointestinal issues and infections.
